components.name: Scroll Area
components.description: Custom scrollable container with a styled 6px-wide scrollbar thumb replacing native browser scrollbars. Vertical (default) or horizontal orientation. Container must have a fixed height to enable scrolling.

examples.name: Type
examples.description: Scrollbar visibility modes — hover (default), always visible, auto on overflow, scroll while scrolling. These are runtime behaviors controlled by the type prop in code.

examples.name: Usage
examples.description: Vertical list scrolling and horizontal tag/card overflow.

props.name: type
props.description: Scrollbar visibility — hover shows on hover, always keeps visible, scroll shows while scrolling, auto shows when overflowing

props.name: className
props.description: Container classes — always include a height constraint (h-48, h-[300px]...) or ScrollArea will not scroll

props.name: orientation (ScrollBar)
props.description: Axis of the scrollbar. Add a second ScrollBar for horizontal scrolling.

bestPractices.do: Always set an explicit height on the ScrollArea container (h-48, h-[300px], or max-h-*). Without a height constraint the container expands to fit content and never scrolls.
bestPractices.dont: Skip the height class and wonder why the scrollbar never appears — ScrollArea requires the parent to be constrained.

bestPractices.do: Add <ScrollBar orientation='horizontal' /> as a child of ScrollArea when content overflows horizontally.
bestPractices.dont: Rely on browser native horizontal scrollbar — it won't appear inside a ScrollArea without an explicit horizontal ScrollBar child.

keyboard.action: Scroll vertically when the scroll area is focused
Scroll by a full page height
Jump to top or bottom of scrollable content

notes: ScrollArea uses overflow-hidden on the root — content remains accessible to screen readers via the Viewport region.
The custom scrollbar is visually positioned but does not interfere with keyboard navigation of inner content.
Focusable elements inside ScrollArea (buttons, links, inputs) remain tab-navigable in DOM order.

related.name: Card
related.desc: For bordered content containers — wrap a ScrollArea inside CardContent for a scrollable card body.

related.name: Table
related.desc: Wrap a Table in a horizontal ScrollArea to handle wide data on small viewports.

related.name: Separator
related.desc: Use inside a vertical ScrollArea to divide list items cleanly.
